description: Lecture on robust price-of-anarchy bounds in smooth games, discussing the derivation of price-of-anarchy bounds and their implications for coarse correlated equilibria.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the derivation of price-of-anarchy bounds, focusing on Nash equilibria and their applications in various game scenarios, including location games and selfish routing games. Key concepts include the definition of price-of-anarchy, the structure of proofs, and the relationship between equilibrium and optimal costs [t=1:84].
